fragility_points: A roughly 4% cushion is meaningful but not lock-safe over several crypto trading days.
Evidence is necessarily concentrated on Binance because the contract is Binance-settled.
key_assumptions: Current BTC strength persists enough that the roughly 3,000-point cushion survives until April 21 noon ET.
No hidden Binance timestamp or operational issue changes the effective observation minute.
Binance-specific pricing does not materially underperform broader BTC pricing at settlement.
main_logical_chain: The governing contract mechanics are clear and point to one specific Binance BTC/USDT one-minute close.
Current Binance price is materially above 72,000, so base directional lean is Yes.
Because the contract is timestamp-specific and exchange-specific, current cushion should be discounted for path risk and operational fragility.
That yields a Yes estimate below the market's 80.5% confidence.
main_thesis: Lean Yes, but below market confidence because current BTC cushion over 72,000 can still fail at the exact Binance noon-ET one-minute close.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) ... has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified
source_quality_view: Primary contract source and direct exchange data are strong, but evidence independence is limited because settlement itself is pinned to Binance.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact future minute, so several days of BTC volatility can still push the decisive close below 72,000.
Binance-specific dislocation or operational issues could settle No even if broader BTC remains firm.
strongest_supports: Polymarket rules clearly define Binance BTC/USDT 1m close at 12:00 ET on April 21 as the settlement condition.
Live Binance BTCUSDT verification on 2026-04-15 showed price around 74,989-75,013, comfortably above 72,000.
A sampled set of recent Binance 1m closes was entirely above 72,000.
timing_relevance: Very high: the market settles on the 12:00 ET one-minute close on 2026-04-21, which maps to 16:00 UTC.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether Binance UI and API presentation of the noon-ET candle are perfectly aligned in practice.
How much short-horizon volatility will rise before April 21.
what_would_change_view: I would move higher if BTC holds a durable multi-day buffer above 72,000 into April 20-21 and Binance timing mechanics remain clean; lower if BTC trades back near the threshold or Binance-specific anomalies emerge.
